blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: We're definitely close. Just about a kilometer more to go...
Just as Amy was about to explain, a ferocious noise erupted from the forest, and a ball of fire shot out.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Ambush! Scatter!
Shirone's party scattered in different directions.
The flame that hit the ground devoured the air, growing in size before dispersing into heat.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Fireball?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Amy! Be careful!
The second and third attacks came simultaneously.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: However, Amy didn't dodge. Instead, she took a battle stance and pierced through the fireballs with an equal number of Flame Strikes.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Show yourself! Cowardly attacking from ambush!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You're quite skilled. It wasn't just luck that brought you this far.
Shirone's party turned to look back at the trail.
A man emerged from the forest, about the same height as Rian but with a noticeably lean and agile build.
Despite having no eyebrows, his slicked-back hair exuded confidence, and his pale complexion resembled that of a serpent.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Who are you?

blocks.speaker: Agado Freeman
blocks.content: The Vice-Captain of the Parrot Mercenary Corps. Agado Freeman.
The word 'Vice-Captain' brought tension to the faces of Shirone's party.
However, it also implied that their destination was not far off.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Shirone, I'll take care of this. When I give the signal, take Rian and Tess to the cliffs.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: That's too dangerous.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: And what about you? Jumping into traps alone, have you forgotten? Are you going to keep underestimating me like this?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's not about underestimating you. He's not alone. There are about 20 of them hiding in the forest.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Amy had already confirmed this through her Spirit Zone, but that was exactly why she wanted to send Shirone ahead.
Being held up here would only give the first checkpoint's reinforcements time to arrive.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: As Amy tried to convince Shirone, she pointed at Freeman.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Shirone, look at that man's weapon. It's a gun.
Shirone too turned to look closely.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: See? You know what a gun is, right? It can reload faster than arrows and is a direct fire weapon. Unlike arrows that explode after impact, it fires in an already activated state, making it specialized for medium to long-range combat. Probably all those hiding in the forest are using this weapon too.
Shirone understood Amy's point.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Alright. I'll leave this to you.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Got it. Rian, Tess, be ready.
Rian followed Shirone's lead without question.
Although it felt wrong to leave a comrade behind, this was not the time for debate, and Tess also braced herself, ready to spring into action.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Now! Go!
Amy shouted with wide eyes, and as Shirone took the lead, Rian and Tess followed on either side.
As expected, flames shot from all directions in the forest, courtesy of the guns.
Amy cast a Fire Wall in a massive circle around her.
The ground bubbled like boiling mud, and flames erupted from beneath her feet, charging fiercely into the forest.
Then, amplifying her mental power, the wall of fire roared, obstructing the enemies' view.
At the same time, Shirone, grabbing Rian and Tess, initiated a spatial transfer.
Although its range was shorter compared to that of a high wizard, it was enough to disappear from the enemies' sight in a blink.
Hearing the noise of the spatial warp, Amy finally ceased casting the Fire Wall and gasped for breath.
The Fire Wall, known as a mana-eating parasite, required continuous mental effort to maintain its barrier.
Despite its powerful effect, it wasn't considered overpowered due to the high cost of casting it.

blocks.speaker: Agado Freeman
blocks.content: Commendable. To sacrifice for your comrades.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Hmph! Sacrifice, my foot. I sent them away because I can handle it myself.

blocks.speaker: Agado Freeman
blocks.content: Is that so? You seem quite exhausted, though. Sorry, but we're in a hurry and can't afford to wait.
As Freeman finished speaking, his subordinates appeared.
The fact that none tried to extinguish the burning forest indicated the absence of mages among them.
However, they were all Schema users, like Freeman, and held guns.
The combination of Schema and magic in combat. Such individuals were known as Gunners on the battlefield.
Having never witnessed a Gunner's battle, Amy could only imagine.
She too could wield long-range magic and Schema simultaneously.

blocks.speaker: Agado Freeman
blocks.content: Sorry, but your reckless adventure ends here.
As Freeman spoke, his subordinates raised their guns.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Only twenty of you for a shootout with me?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Amy concentrated, drawing her legs together as if channeling strength to her lower abdomen, and began to cast a spell.
A whirlwind swirled around her, lifting fist-sized stones into the air around her, which then ignited with a whooshing sound.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: You've picked the wrong opponent this time.
Amy's eyes blazed red.
Even as she ran, Tess kept looking back.
Though she trusted and followed Shirone's lead, the plan seemed reckless.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Gunners might be weaker in firepower compared to mages, but they matched swordsmen in physical prowess.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: The thought of Amy fighting among them made Tess's stomach clench.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Shirone, shouldn't we go back now? I'm worried about Amy.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It'll be fine. Delaying only puts us at a disadvantage. We might be pursued by the forces that survived the first checkpoint. Our priority is to reach the hideout as quickly as possible.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: But what about Amy? Wouldn't it have been better if you took out the enemies and came back?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's not like that. A mage's strength isn't simply measured by their power. It varies with the situation and the type of magic. Against Gunners, Amy is far stronger than I am.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Tess seemed unconvinced.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: She knew Amy was a senior student, but the feats Shirone had performed since arriving were nothing short of incredible.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Is that possible? No matter how skilled, there's something to be said for numerical superiority.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Tess, thinking like a true swordsman, placed emphasis on physical strength.
Indeed, in battle, the total physical force represented by numbers is a critical factor.
However, when it comes to magic, the scenario shifts slightly.
For mages, who manipulate countless phenomena through the combination of knowledge and omnipotence, the number of opponents isn't as critical.
What matters more to them is the ability to manipulate situations to maximize the efficiency of their finely honed skills.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The properties of photons differ from those of flames. Moreover, the strategies of the Four Symbols are distinct. Amy specializes in long-range magic, maximizing the advantages of her Red Eyes. No matter how strong the enemies in the forest might be, if Amy confronts them in her element...

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No matter their numbers, they stand no chance against Amy.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Tess swallowed hard.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Normally, she felt like she was looking after a stubborn younger sibling when dealing with Shirone and Amy, but in the heat of battle, they turned as cold and calculating as ice.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: You guys are truly amazing. How can you be so resolute?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: That's a misunderstanding on your part. If anyone's amazing, it's you, Tess.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Tch, what have I done? You were the one who breached the fortress, and I didn't even lend a hand.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The paths of swordsmen and mages are inherently different from the start.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: While the mental arts can see individuals surpass age-related expectations, physical growth supports the swordsman's path, typically leading to their prominence emerging somewhat later.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Shirone, we've arrived.
Emerging from the forest, they were greeted by the sight of the vast blue sea churning below a magnificent cliff.
To the east of the cliff, a brick house was visible, likely their hideout.
Yet, reaching it required passing one final obstacle.
Squad Leader Falcoa awaited Shirone's party.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Given her assessment of his character, Falcoa should have long since charged into battle.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: What's going on? He's still here?

blocks.speaker: Falcoa
blocks.content: Kek, smashing you right from the start would have been fun. But, I have my orders.
Falcoa's compliance was driven by Marsha's directives.
Although it went against his temperament, he followed them, for it was she who transformed him from a societal pariah into a hero of the battlefield.

blocks.speaker: Falcoa
blocks.content: Well, you've made it this far. The leader was right. The woman you're looking for is inside that building. Try going in and taking her out.
Falcoa mockingly stepped aside, but Shirone's group remained stationary.
The lethal aura that had been absent in the royal palace now barred their way, dense and overwhelming.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Tess empathized with Amy's position. Facing a master of such caliber necessitated a level of risk-taking from their side as well.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: We're being baited. I'll handle this with Rian. Shirone, go inside and bring Yuna back.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Alright.
Shirone nodded without hesitation, understanding the plan.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Tess realized why Amy had smiled; rather than feeling slighted, she felt a warmth of trust.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: 'So this cold world has its warmth, too. Is this what being trusted feels like?'
With Shirone's skills, escaping alone was hardly a challenge.
After a brief pause, he executed a spatial warp, vanishing in a flash of light.
Yet, Falcoa didn't even glance in his direction.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Tess realized then that Falcoa had intended to let Shirone pass from the beginning.

blocks.speaker: Falcoa
blocks.content: Why?

blocks.speaker: Falcoa
blocks.content: Well, I have my orders from the leader, and frankly, I prefer the company of the ladies.
Drawing his sword, Falcoa pointed it at Tess.
His murky aura was unmistakable, yet the drunken instability was gone.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: He's a Schema user. I'll hold him off; you attack.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: That won't do. I'll defend; you attack.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Are you crazy? How could you possibly hold him off? I hate to say it, but you can't handle him alone.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: If it's impossible, then so be it.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: What?

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Tess was incredulously wide-eyed, but Rian was serious, not showing any sign of giving up the fight, merely standing his ground.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: We're here to defeat him, not just to survive. Fighting just to save our own skins isn't the point.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Tess couldn't argue.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: You might buy us time, but you know it's not enough to win. We'd both end up dead. So, I'll hold him off. You focus on the attack.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Rian's logic was sound.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Sharpening the blade, not fortifying the shield, was the essence of victory, even at the cost of life.
